title: More Nonsense Limerick 36
content:
There was an old person of Woking,
    Whose mind was perverse and provoking;
    He sate on a rail, 
    With his head in a pail,
    That illusive old person of Woking.
meaning: About: Edward Lear was an English artist, illustrator and writer known for his literary nonsense, in poetry and prose, and especially his limericks, a form which he popularised.
